steps.title: Focus on the narrator's attitude
steps.content: The passage briefly explains how the piano arrived, but its main emphasis is the narrator's reaction. She says she had to act happy but hated the piano, then imagines it glaring at her and wanting to be free.

steps.title: Reject plot-only readings
steps.content: A reverses the narrator's attitude; she did not want the piano near her bedroom. C invents information about her brothers' talent. D is too narrow because the purchase only sets up the narrator's feelings. The answer is B.
